angle: Variation 1 — photo full-bleed; headline in a color-block field tucked into the LOWER-LEFT corner (roughly 40% width × 25% height), pulling a brand-palette hex that complements the photo's dominant hue. Support line immediately under the block. Asymmetric restraint.
subject: Lobster Roll
support: Toasted brioche. Cream sauce. Chives. Charred lemon.
prompt:
A social media image for Jackson House, an American tavern. Brand palette: forest green #1F3B2D, oxblood #5B1A1A, cream #F2EADF, walnut brown #3B2A1E, mustard #C8962F. Typography: old-style or slab serif headlines (wood-type character) paired with condensed all-caps grotesque labels, thin hairline rules. Texture: cream laid paper, letterpress emboss, film grain on photography. Photography style: warm tungsten candlelight 2800K, shallow depth of field, human hands in frame, Portra 800 film look, documentary feel. Symmetric menu-card compositions alternating with asymmetric editorial photography.

FORMAT: Editorial hero dish spotlight. Single plate, asymmetric crop, natural light, restrained type. One focal point on a rule-of-thirds intersection, one supporting beat, one resting zone. 60–65% negative space for Blue Mezcal; 55–75% for Jackson House; 75–95% canvas fill for Azteca.

COMPOSITION: rule-of-thirds focal point, asymmetric composition, 4:5 portrait; clean negative space in the upper third for headline

HEADLINE (quote exactly as written, no paraphrasing, no misspellings): "Lobster Roll"
SUPPORT LINE (quote exactly): "Toasted brioche. Cream sauce. Chives. Charred lemon."
ADDRESS / CTA (small, bottom margin, low-key): "17 Wood St · Middletown, DE — Reserve your table · link in bio"
WORDMARK (small, one corner, 40–80px equivalent, 50–70% opacity): "JACKSON HOUSE"

VARIATION DIRECTION: Variation 1 — photo full-bleed; headline in a color-block field tucked into the LOWER-LEFT corner (roughly 40% width × 25% height), pulling a brand-palette hex that complements the photo's dominant hue. Support line immediately under the block. Asymmetric restraint.

PHOTO RULE: HERO-PHOTO RULE (HIGHEST PRIORITY — cannot be violated):
  1. The reference food photograph supplied as input is the CANVAS FOUNDATION of this      design. It fills the primary region of the composition at FULL SCALE. It is NEVER      reduced to a small swatch, tile, inset, thumbnail, or decorative element. The photo      is the hero; the template is LAID OVER IT.
  2. Typography, colour fields, motifs, and ornament sit OVER or ADJACENT TO the full-     size photo — they do not shrink the photo to make room. If a headline needs space,      use an upper-third or lower-third color-block field ON TOP of the photo, or extend      negative brand-color margin from one edge of the photo outward (photo still fills      60–85% of canvas minimum).
  3. DO NOT regenerate, restyle, or alter the plating, lighting, colours, or contents      of the food in any way. The photo must remain photographically identical to the source.
  4. Variations that shrink, miniaturize, inset, or tile the photo are REJECTED.      (User feedback, BM-A1-002: "I don't want smaller images of what we have, use      the real images as the template, and design OVER them.")

PHOTO-LED PALETTE COMPLEMENT RULE:
  The brand-palette colours you choose for headline fields, accent blocks, and type   MUST COMPLEMENT the photo's dominant hue, not clash with it. If the photo is warm-  dominant (amber, orange, red, bourbon), pull cooler brand colours (cobalt, deep   navy, forest) for contrast. If the photo is cool-dominant (blue, green, grey), pull   warmer brand colours (brass, oxblood, marigold). Name the photo's dominant hue in   your head first, then pick the brand-palette hex that complements it.

FINAL: Render at the aspect ratio specified via the API (not in prose). Dense typography with purpose. Real brand register. No stock gradients, no Canva-template feel. Avoid the AI tells: no cyan-to-magenta gradients, no default Poppins/Montserrat/Open Sans, no 33/33/33 colour balance, no centered dead-symmetric layouts unless ceremonial, no default 5px 90° drop shadows. Texture with intent: noise/grain overlay, paper, halftone, risograph where register calls for it.

angle: Variation 2 — photo anchors the LOWER TWO-THIRDS at full scale; the UPPER THIRD is a quiet extended brand-palette margin (complementary hex) carrying the headline + sub in an editorial magazine-spread rhythm. Hairline brand-palette rule separates the two zones. [Winner pattern from BM-A1-002.]

Texture: cream laid paper, letterpress emboss, film grain on photography. Photography style: warm tungsten candlelight 2800K, shallow depth of field, human hands in frame, Portra 800 film look, documentary feel. Symmetric menu-card compositions alternating with asymmetric editorial photography.

FORMAT: Editorial hero dish spotlight. Single plate, asymmetric crop, natural light, restrained type. One focal point on a rule-of-thirds intersection, one supporting beat, one resting zone. 60–65% negative space for Blue Mezcal; 55–75% for Jackson House; 75–95% canvas fill for Azteca.

COMPOSITION: rule-of-thirds focal point, asymmetric composition, 4:5 portrait; clean negative space in the upper third for headline

HEADLINE (quote exactly as written, no paraphrasing, no misspellings): "Lobster Roll"
SUPPORT LINE (quote exactly): "Toasted brioche. Cream sauce. Chives. Charred lemon."
ADDRESS / CTA (small, bottom margin, low-key): "17 Wood St · Middletown, DE — Reserve your table · link in bio"
WORDMARK (small, one corner, 40–80px equivalent, 50–70% opacity): "JACKSON HOUSE"

VARIATION DIRECTION: Variation 8 — photo full-bleed; headline in a color-block field in the UPPER-RIGHT corner, wordmark anchored bottom-left, hairline rule connecting the two. Mirrored composition to V1.

PHOTO RULE: HERO-PHOTO RULE (HIGHEST PRIORITY — cannot be violated):
  1. The reference food photograph supplied as input is the CANVAS FOUNDATION of this      design. It fills the primary region of the composition at FULL SCALE. It is NEVER      reduced to a small swatch, tile, inset, thumbnail, or decorative element. The photo      is the hero; the template is LAID OVER IT.
  2. Typography, colour fields, motifs, and ornament sit OVER or ADJACENT TO the full-     size photo — they do not shrink the photo to make room. If a headline needs space,      use an upper-third or lower-third color-block field ON TOP of the photo, or extend      negative brand-color margin from one edge of the photo outward (photo still fills      60–85% of canvas minimum).
  3. DO NOT regenerate, restyle, or alter the plating, lighting, colours, or contents      of the food in any way. The photo must remain photographically identical to the source.
  4. Variations that shrink, miniaturize, inset, or tile the photo are REJECTED.      (User feedback, BM-A1-002: "I don't want smaller images of what we have, use      the real images as the template, and design OVER them.")

PHOTO-LED PALETTE COMPLEMENT RULE:
  The brand-palette colours you choose for headline fields, accent blocks, and type   MUST COMPLEMENT the photo's dominant hue, not clash with it. If the photo is warm-  dominant (amber, orange, red, bourbon), pull cooler brand colours (cobalt, deep   navy, forest) for contrast. If the photo is cool-dominant (blue, green, grey), pull   warmer brand colours (brass, oxblood, marigold). Name the photo's dominant hue in   your head first, then pick the brand-palette hex that complements it.

FINAL: Render at the aspect ratio specified via the API (not in prose). Dense typography with purpose. Real brand register. No stock gradients, no Canva-template feel. Avoid the AI tells: no cyan-to-magenta gradients, no default Poppins/Montserrat/Open Sans, no 33/33/33 colour balance, no centered dead-symmetric layouts unless ceremonial, no default 5px 90° drop shadows. Texture with intent: noise/grain overlay, paper, halftone, risograph where register calls for it.
